species.mnemonic: Cheerily, cheer-up, cheerily — a caroling whistle at dawn and dusk

sound_types.song: Rich caroling whistle, phrases of 2-3 syllables with rising and falling pitch
sound_types.call: Sharp tut-tut-tut alarm; also a thin, high seep in flight

species.confuser_notes: Varied Thrush has a single eerie buzzy note on one pitch, not a caroling phrase

species.mnemonic: Song: clear whistled fee-bee (2 notes, second lower). Call: chick-a-dee-dee-dee

species.mnemonic: Drink-your-teeeee — two short intro notes then a buzzy trill

species.mnemonic: Long warbling jumble ending with a harsh buzzy zhreee — sounds happy but messy

species.mnemonic: Loud wick-wick-wick-wick (like rapid laughter) or a single loud kyeer

sound_types.song: Loud, rapid wick-wick-wick-wick lasting several seconds

species.confuser_notes: Pileated is louder, slower, and has a wilder irregular rhythm; Flicker is evenly spaced

species.mnemonic: Harsh shack-shack-shack — also a great mimic (imitates Red-tailed Hawk screams)

species.mnemonic: Scratchy, buzzy, metallic series of chips and squeals — surprisingly loud for its size

sound_types.song: Complex scratchy buzzing and squealing song, often from an exposed perch

species.confuser_notes: Rufous (seasonal visitor) has a simpler, lower-pitched buzz; Anna's song is longer and more complex

species.mnemonic: Faster, higher-pitched tsee-dee-dee than Black-capped — like a sped-up version

sound_types.song: Less distinct than Black-capped; doesn't give the clear fee-bee whistle
sound_types.call: Rapid, high tsee-dee-dee, faster and buzzier than Black-capped

species.confuser_notes: Key confuser pair — Black-capped is slower with clear fee-bee song; Chestnut-backed is higher and faster

species.confuser_notes: Kinglet is even higher-pitched with a sibilant see-see-see; Bushtit is slightly lower with a harder tsit quality

species.mnemonic: Chattering mimic — whistles, clicks, squeals, and borrowed songs all jumbled together

sound_types.song: Long rambling series of whistles, clicks, rattles, and mimicked sounds
sound_types.call: Descending whistle; also a harsh chatter

species.confuser_notes: Mockingbird (rare in Seattle) repeats phrases 3+ times; Starling jumbles everything without repeating

confuser_pairs.label: Black-capped vs Chestnut-backed Chickadee
confuser_pairs.key_difference: Black-capped: slower dee-dee-dee + clear fee-bee song. Chestnut-backed: faster, higher, no fee-bee.

confuser_pairs.label: Song Sparrow vs Golden-crowned Sparrow
confuser_pairs.key_difference: Song Sparrow: 3 notes + complex trill. Golden-crowned: 3 pure descending whistles, no trill.

confuser_pairs.label: American Crow vs Steller's Jay
confuser_pairs.key_difference: Crow: clean caw-caw. Jay: harsh shack-shack, more grating and varied.

confuser_pairs.label: Dark-eyed Junco vs Spotted Towhee
confuser_pairs.key_difference: Junco: even trill on one pitch. Towhee: 2 intro notes THEN a trill.

confuser_pairs.label: House Finch vs Song Sparrow
confuser_pairs.key_difference: House Finch: continuous warble ending in buzz. Song Sparrow: structured 3-note opening.

lesson_plan.description: 5 lessons of 3 birds each, ordered by distinctiveness (most distinctive first)

lessons.title: The unmistakable three
lessons.rationale: Loud, distinctive, impossible to confuse with each other. Builds confidence.

lessons.title: Backyard singers
lessons.rationale: Classic songs you'll hear every morning. Introduces the fee-bee mnemonic.

lessons.title: The sparrow start
lessons.rationale: First confuser challenge — three sparrow-like species with distinct songs.

lessons.title: Small and buzzy
lessons.rationale: Complex songs that reward repeated listening. Wren vs finch structure.

lessons.title: Flock sounds and mimics
lessons.rationale: Contact calls and mimicry. Introduces the chickadee confuser pair.
